vulnerability.description: jwt-go allows access restriction bypass because m["aud"] mishandling in token validation. The library does not properly validate the audience claim in JWT tokens, potentially allowing tokens intended for one service to be accepted by another.
vulnerability.note: The dgrijalva/jwt-go library is deprecated and unmaintained. The community fork golang-jwt/jwt is the official replacement.

dependency_chain.description: UNUSUAL CASE: github.com/dgrijalva/jwt-go v3.2.0+incompatible is declared as a DIRECT dependency in go.mod (line 40, first require block, no '// indirect' comment). However, it is NOT present in go.sum and NO Go source files (.go) import it. The project exclusively uses the modern replacement github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5 v5.3.1 (imported in 10 source files). This is a phantom/orphan dependency -- likely a leftover from before the migration to golang-jwt/jwt that was never cleaned from go.mod. Go 1.17+ module graph pruning means it does not get downloaded or affect the build, but it remains declared.

filippo_edwards25519.name: filippo.io/edwards25519
filippo_edwards25519.note: edwards25519 is a well-known transitive dependency of go-sql-driver/mysql for Ed25519 authentication support. It may also be pulled by github.com/microsoft/go-mssqldb@v1.9.6.

sbom_locations.sbom_file: not generated (Go project - use 'syft' or 'cyclonedx-gomod' to generate)
sbom_locations.note: The dependency is declared in go.mod but pruned by Go 1.17+ module graph pruning since no package from this module is imported. A CycloneDX or SPDX SBOM generated from the compiled binary would NOT include this module. However, an SBOM generated from go.mod parsing alone would include it.

dfs_tree_context.description: DFS tree levels showing the dependency hierarchy. jwt-go is unique because it is a depth-1 (direct) phantom dependency, not a true transitive chain.

levels.type: leaf (VULNERABLE, phantom - not in go.sum, not imported)
levels.note: This module has no transitive children because it is never actually resolved/downloaded due to Go module pruning.

comparison_with_edwards25519_chain.description: For contrast, here is the edwards25519 chain which IS a true transitive dependency actually present in the build

comparison_with_go_git_chain.description: go-git has the deepest and most complex transitive chain in this project

levels.type: direct dependency (22+ source files import it)

remediation.recommendation: Remove github.com/dgrijalva/jwt-go from go.mod. It is a phantom dependency: declared but never imported or downloaded. The project already uses the secure replacement github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5@v5.3.1.
remediation.command: Run 'go mod tidy' to automatically remove the unused dependency from go.mod
remediation.risk: None - no code imports this module
remediation.historical_context: jwt-go was likely required by an older version of github.com/markbates/goth or another OAuth library. The goth library (v1.82.0) has since migrated to golang-jwt/jwt. The orphan entry in go.mod was never cleaned up.
